title: hieracium umbellatum subsp. brevifolium
intro: Hieracium umbellatum subsp. brevifolium, also known as the narrow-leaved hawk's-beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is said to have medicinal benefits such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Additionally, the plant's roots have been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions and digestive problems.
